methods.description: This method explores the effect of seasonal temperatures on the compositional variability of snail data using principal coordinates analysis (PCoA) and canonical correspondence analysis (CCA).

steps.description: PCoA based on Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distances was conducted to explore the entire compositional variation among the samples.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCoA using Bray-Curtis dissimilarity to analyze compositional variation

steps.description: Target environmental variables were correlated to the ordination space to determine their association with the main compositional turnover.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate environmental variables with ordination space

steps.description: Detrended correspondence analysis (DCA) was used to calculate the gradient length of compositional turnover in standard deviation (SD) units.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DCA to calculate gradient length in SD units

steps.description: CCA analysis was undertaken, and variables were selected after removing high multicollinearity until the Variance Inflation Factors (VIFs) were low.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CCA analysis with low VIF variables

steps.description: A combined model comprising a forward selection model was used to determine the uncorrelated variation, and a single model to test each variable separately.
steps.searchable_summary: use forward selection model to determine uncorrelated variation

steps.description: The statistical significance of each variable was evaluated by Monte Carlo permutation tests with 999 permutations.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Monte Carlo permutation tests for statistical significance
